mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits via lower discount rates and easier financial conditions, lifting S&P 500 prices over the next month. The signal is valid when policy rates are still near zero and the move reflects an ongoing easing path, not a one-off noise print.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are negative in a majority of periods when FEDFUNDS is declining, or if the rolling 2-year rank correlation is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL inflation accelerates above recent trend, real yields and expected policy restraint rise, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL rises more than 0.3% month-over-month and the 3-month annualized inflation rate is above its 6-month average
failure_condition: Reject if inflation acceleration episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the sign of the rolling 3-year effect turns positive.

mechanism_narrative: A rising UNRATE accompanied by slowing PAYEMS indicates labor-market deterioration and weaker household income expectations, which should pressure S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by at least 0.2 percentage points over 2 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered labor deterioration episodes do not produce negative 20-day returns relative to baseline, or if PAYEMS trends higher despite rising unemployment and the signal still predicts declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp drop in RSAFS signals weakening retail demand and softer near-term revenue growth for consumer-facing firms, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if large RSAFS declines are followed by positive 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the short-horizon effect disappears in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO combined with rising HOUST indicates improving real activity and supply-side momentum, supporting earnings expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Reject if joint increases in INDPRO and HOUST fail to precede positive 60-day SPX returns, or if either series alone explains the effect and the interaction adds no incremental predictive value.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in quarterly GDPC1 below its recent run rate signals broad demand weakening and lower forward earnings, which should produce negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth is below its prior 4-quarter average
failure_condition: Reject if GDP slowdown episodes are not followed by negative 60-day SPX returns on average, or if the sign remains negative even when GDP growth reaccelerates.

mechanism_narrative: If PAYEMS growth turns negative while UNRATE is still stable, the market should anticipate a delayed labor deterioration and price lower S&P 500 returns over the next month.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS declines month-over-month while UNRATE is unchanged or rises by less than 0.1 percentage points
failure_condition: Reject if PAYEMS contractions with flat unemployment do not precede negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the effect is absent once UNRATE begins rising.

mechanism_narrative: In a tightening cycle, a rising FEDFUNDS rate paired with elevated CPIAUCSL should increase real-rate pressure and reduce equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS is above 2.0% and CPIAUCSL inflation is above its 6-month moving average
failure_condition: Reject if high-rate/high-inflation tightening episodes fail to produce negative 20-day SPX returns, or if the signal flips positive during tightening regimes.
